FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class EditDocument

Namespace
FluentGpu.Text
Assembly
FluentGpu.Text.dll

The text-edit document: a gap buffer of UTF-16 code units (grow by doubling, minimum capacity 64). Every public index is a UTF-16 code-unit offset — the unit WinUI exposes through SelectionStart/MaxLength — so the control layer never converts. Grapheme safety is layered ON TOP via the boundary scanners (SnapToGrapheme(int)/NextGrapheme(int)/PrevGrapheme(int), BCL UAX#29 segmentation — covers surrogate pairs, combining marks, ZWJ emoji, regional indicators): callers that move a caret must land on those boundaries, while the raw Insert(int, ReadOnlySpan<char>)/Remove(int, int) primitives deliberately accept any code-unit range so undo replay can restore exact historic states.

Hard line breaks are a single '\r' (WinUI normalizes CRLF/LF on the way in — see NormalizeNewlines(string)); '\n' must never be stored. The boundary scanners exploit that invariant: a position right after '\r' is always a grapheme boundary (UAX#29 GB4: break after a control), so they anchor their forward walk at the surrounding line start instead of offset 0 — O(line length), and text fields are short.

public sealed class EditDocument

object

Constructors

EditDocument() 

public EditDocument()

Properties

this[int]

Random access to one code unit (logical index — the gap is invisible to callers).

public char this[int index] { get; }

Parameters

index int

Property Value

char

Length

Document length in UTF-16 code units.

public int Length { get; }

Property Value

int

Version

Bumps on every content mutation (insert/remove/reset). Render/measure caches key off this so a stale shaped layout can never be replayed against edited text.

public int Version { get; }

Property Value

int

Methods

AsSpan()

Contiguous view of the whole document, obtained by MOVING THE GAP TO THE END first (one memmove of the post-gap tail). The BCL segmentation APIs need one contiguous span, and a two-segment scanner would have to re-implement UAX#29 stitching across the seam — moving the gap is simpler and provably correct. Repeated scans after the move are free; the next interior Insert(int, ReadOnlySpan<char>)/Remove(int, int) pays the move back. That trade is deliberate: every caller is a cold user-gesture path (caret nav, word jump, grapheme snap), never the frame loop, and edit fields are short.

public ReadOnlySpan<char> AsSpan()

Returns

ReadOnlySpan<char>

CopyTo(int, int, Span<char>)

Copy a range into dst without disturbing the gap (alloc-free read path).

public void CopyTo(int start, int length, Span<char> dst)

Parameters

start int
length int
dst Span<char>

GetText()

Materialize the whole document as a string (user-gesture edge only — copy, undo capture, GetText).

public string GetText()

Returns

string

GetText(int, int)

Materialize a range as a string (user-gesture edge only).

public string GetText(int start, int length)

Parameters

start int
length int

Returns

string

Insert(int, ReadOnlySpan<char>)

Insert text at pos. An empty insert is not a mutation (no Version bump). The caller owns newline normalization — '\n' must never reach the buffer.

public void Insert(int pos, ReadOnlySpan<char> text)

Parameters

pos int
text ReadOnlySpan<char>

LineEnd(int)

The end of the line containing pos: the index of the following '\r' (the caret sits BEFORE the break, like WinUI End), or Length.

public int LineEnd(int pos)

Parameters

pos int

Returns

int

LineStart(int)

The start of the line containing pos: the index after the preceding '\r', or 0.

public int LineStart(int pos)

Parameters

pos int

Returns

int

NextGrapheme(int)

The grapheme boundary after pos — the caret target for a Right arrow. From the middle of a cluster this lands at the END of that cluster (never inside one).

public int NextGrapheme(int pos)

Parameters

pos int

Returns

int

NextWord(int)

Ctrl+Right target: skip the run under the caret (word chars, or a punctuation run), THEN trailing whitespace — landing at the START of the next word, exactly like the Windows TextBox. From inside whitespace only the whitespace is skipped.

public int NextWord(int pos)

Parameters

pos int

Returns

int

NormalizeNewlines(ReadOnlySpan<char>)

Span form of NormalizeNewlines(string) — always materializes a string (cold paste edge).

public static string NormalizeNewlines(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)

Parameters

text ReadOnlySpan<char>

Returns

string

NormalizeNewlines(string)

Map CRLF and lone LF to the document's '\r' form (WinUI's TextBox normalization). Returns the input instance unchanged when it contains no '\n' — the overwhelmingly common case costs one IndexOf.

public static string NormalizeNewlines(string text)

Parameters

text string

Returns

string

PrevGrapheme(int)

The grapheme boundary before pos — the caret target for a Left arrow (and the range start for a Backspace). From the middle of a cluster this lands at the START of that cluster.

public int PrevGrapheme(int pos)

Parameters

pos int

Returns

int

PrevWord(int)

Ctrl+Left target: skip whitespace backward, then the word (or punctuation) run — landing at the start of the current word when mid-word, or the start of the previous word when at a word start.

public int PrevWord(int pos)

Parameters

pos int

Returns

int

Remove(int, int)

Remove length code units at start by absorbing them into the gap (no copying of the removed range). A zero-length remove is not a mutation.

public void Remove(int start, int length)

Parameters

start int
length int

Reset(ReadOnlySpan<char>)

Replace the entire content (programmatic Text set). Keeps the existing buffer when it fits; grows by doubling otherwise. Bumps Version. Caller owns newline normalization.

public void Reset(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)

Parameters

text ReadOnlySpan<char>

SnapToGrapheme(int)

Snap pos DOWN to the nearest grapheme boundary at or before it (clamped to [0, Length]). A position already on a boundary is returned unchanged.

public int SnapToGrapheme(int pos)

Parameters

pos int

Returns

int
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
